data.title: Qinetiq Sees About 3% Organic Revenue Growth In FY26
data.description:
Qinetiq Group PLC :

  • QINETIQ GROUP PLC - TRADING UPDATE

  • QINETIQ GROUP PLC: CONTINUE TO EXPECT TO DELIVER ABOUT 3% ORGANIC REVENUE GROWTH, AN OPERATING MARGIN OF ABOUT 11% FOR FY 2026

  • QINETIQ GROUP PLC: NEAR-TERM TRADING ENVIRONMENT IN OUR CORE MARKETS REMAINS CHALLENGING

  • QINETIQ GROUP PLC: IN TERMS OF RESTRUCTURING WE ARE NOW DRIVING THROUGH AND EMBEDDING THE CHANGES UNDERTAKEN IN H1

  • QINETIQ GROUP PLC: RESTRUCTURING INCLUDES ALIGNMENT OF THE US BUSINESS TO THE NATIONAL DEFENCE AND SECURITY PRIORITIES OF THE US ADMINISTRATION

  • QINETIQ GROUP PLC: RESTRUCTURING INCLUDES RIGHT-SIZING OF THE AUSTRALIA BUSINESS AND STREAMLINING OUR UK OPERATIONS

  • QINETIQ GROUP PLC: TOTAL ORDER INTAKE NOW STANDS AT MORE THAN £1.3 BILLION

data.title: India's Aarti Surfactants surges after Q3 profit growth
data.description:
** Shares of Aarti Surfactants advance 20% to 459.1 rupees

** Co reported an over four-fold y/y rise in Q3 consol net profit to 36.6 mln rupees ($402,103.68); rev up 27.6%

** Stock on track to gain for 3rd straight day

** More than 259,000 shares traded as of 1:08 P.M. IST, 28.7x their 30-day moving avg; busiest day yet since Sept. 18, 2024

** AARU fell 39.2% in 2025

($1 = 91.0213 Indian rupees)

data.title: India's Deepak Nitrite rises on commissioning plant in Gujarat
data.description:
** Shares of Deepak Nitrite up 4% to 1596 rupees

** Chemicals maker commissioned its nitration and second hydrogenation plant at Dahej, Gujarat, with total capex of 850 mln rupees ($9.3 mln)

** Stock set to snap seven straight sessions of losses

** More than 1.2 mln shares traded, 8x their 30-day moving avg

** Shares changed hands most in a session since May 29, 2025

** Avg rating of the stock is "hold" and median PT is 1,826.5 rupees, ~15% higher than current price - data compiled by LSEG

** DPNT fell 30.6% in 2025

($1 = 91.0080 Indian rupees)

Disclosure of dealings and positions in this offeror is not required

Notes:

1.

Rule 8 of the Takeover Code (the "Code") sets out the circumstances in which Dealing Disclosures and/or Opening Position Disclosures are required to be made by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them, persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more and exempt principal traders ("EPTs") connected with the parties to an offer.

2.

Where an offeror is marked "Disclosure of dealings and positions in this offeror is not required", dealings and positions in relevant securities of that offeror are not required to be disclosed under Rule 8. This is either because it has been announced that the offer or possible offer is, or is likely to be, solely in cash or because disclosure is not required for some other reason.

3.

Dealings and positions required to be disclosed under Rule 8 of the Code include dealings and positions in derivatives referenced to, and options in respect of, relevant securities of an offeree company and, where appropriate, an offeror (see Note 2 above).

4.

The information set out in the Disclosure Table relating to the relevant securities in issue of an offeree company or of an offeror (and any amendments thereto) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by such company under Rule 2.9 of the Code. Similarly, details of any deadline set under Rule 2.6 by which a potential offeror must announce either a firm intention to make a bid or that it does not intend to make an offer (or, in the case of Rule 2.6(a), by which the Panel must consent to an extension of the deadline) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by the offeree company.

5.

Additions and deletions of offeree companies and offerors are reflected in the Disclosure Table throughout the day, following the relevant announcement. Other amendments may also be made throughout the day. In any event, details of the classes of relevant securities issued by an offeree or offeror company, the number of such securities in issue ("NSI") and the International Securities Identification Number ("ISIN") of such securities announced under Rule 2.9 of the Code before 5.00 pm each day will be included in the final version of the Disclosure Table by 5.30 pm that day. The final version of the Disclosure Table issued each day will be marked "Final" and persons required to make Dealing Disclosures and Opening Position Disclosures are advised to use that final version. Any changes to the Rule 2.6 deadline of a potential offeror will also be reflected in the "Final" version of the Disclosure Table, although any such changes will not be shown in the "Amendments" section at the top of the Disclosure Table.

6.

Dealing Disclosures by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them and connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the business day (as defined in the Code) following the date of the relevant dealing and Dealing Disclosures by persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by 3.30 pm on the business day following the date of the relevant dealing.

7.

Opening Position Disclosures by the parties to an offer and by connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Where required, Opening Position Disclosures by persons with interests in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by no later than 3.30 pm on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Details of the time and date at which an offer period commenced and at which an offeror was first identified are set out in the Disclosure Table. In order to calculate the deadline for Opening Position Disclosures, it is therefore necessary to count forward 10 business days from the relevant date.

8.

Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service ("RIS").

9.

This Note is applicable if it says "See Note 9 below" by the name of the company in the Disclosure Table above.  The Executive has granted the company a dispensation from the requirements under the Code that announcements must be published via a RIS. The company is instead required to publish all announcements on its website. The Executive has also granted a dispensation from Note 3 on Rule 8 of the Code that disclosures made under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a RIS. Therefore, Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code may be made to the company at the address stated in the announcement that commenced the offer period.

10.

The following are approved Regulatory Information Services:

.

Business Wire Regulatory Disclosure provided by Business Wire

+44 (0)20 7626 1982

data.title: India stocks, rupee, bonds, swaps, call at 1:00 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex fell 0.53% to 82,805, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 index lost 0.6% to 25,431, led by information technology firms on mixed earnings, with global trade uncertainty and persistent foreign fund outflows keeping overall sentiment subdued.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee fell 0.13% versus the U.S. dollar to 91.0250, with ongoing flow-based dollar demand tightening the squeeze as the currency broke past 91 per dollar levels for first time in a month, putting it within striking distance of a fresh record low.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN064835G=CC) was quoted at 98.6850 rupees, with the yield about 2.5 bps lower at 6.6607%, due to growing bets of likely central bank buying in the secondary market.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) inched lower to 5.56%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rose slightly to 6.08%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.45% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.00%

data.title: India's GoodEnough Energy commissions country's largest battery storage system
data.description:
India's GoodEnough Energy said on Tuesday that it has commissioned a 7 Gigawatt-hour (GWh) battery energy storage system (BESS), the country's largest, in the northern state of Uttar Pradesh.

The company, founded in 2023 with an initial investment of 4.5 billion rupees ($49.4 million), plans to expand to make 25 GWh of BESS within three years, it said.

Indian companies largely depend on imports from China for battery storage systems for clean energy projects.

India is targetting 500 GW of non‑fossil power capacity by 2030 and requires an estimated 230 GWh of storage to stabilise its rapidly expanding green grid.

In 2023, the country approved 94 billion rupees of funding to make BESS equipment, providing financial support of up to 40% of the capital cost.

($1 = 91.0213 Indian rupees)

data.title: Total flags wider quarterly refining margins
data.description:
French oil major TotalEnergies expects lower oil and liquefied natural gas sales in the fourth quarter of 2025, but stronger downstream results, as higher margins on refining fuels partially offset lower crude prices

The company's European refining margin marker stood at $85.7 per metric ton in the fourth quarter of 2025, up 231% from the year prior.

data.title: Poland - Factors to Watch Jan 20
data.description:
The following are significant news stories, press reports and events on Tuesday. ALL TIMES GMT (Poland: GMT + 2 hours):

BUDGET

Polish President Karol Nawrocki said on X on Monday that he had signed the 2026 budget but sent it to the constitutional tribunal for checks that it adheres to the law, citing concern over the deficit and rising debt.

GAZA BOARD OF PEACE

Polish President Karol Nawrocki has been invited to join the Gaza Board of Peace by U.S. President Donald Trump, the Polish president's foreign policy adviser said on Monday.

MERCOSUR

Polish exporters of car parts, the parmaceutical and cosmetics sectors could benefit from the Mercosur trade deal, Rzeczpospolita daily reported.

ELECTRIC CARS

The value of applications to the electric car subsidy programme 'NaszEauto" has reached 92.5% of the available funds, Dziennnik Gazeta Prawna reported.

ECONOMIC GROWTH

Poland's economy will grow by 3.5% in 2026, according to the latest World Economic Outlook published by the International Monetary Funf (MFW), Puls Biznesu daily reported.

****Reuters has not verified stories reported by Polish media and does not vouch for their accuracy.****

data.title: Salzgitter gains from German stimulus and EU tariff plans, says Oddo
data.description:
** Oddo BHF upgrades German steelmaker Salzgitter to "outperform" from "underperform", citing the company's potential to benefit from the German stimulus plan and the strengthening of trade barriers in Europe

** Improved prices thanks to the prospect of higher tariffs already benefit Salzgitter, with the EU's planned trade defence tool likely to have an even stronger effect from July 1, Oddo says

** There are signs of demand recovery, and the broker says Salzgitter's exposure to infrastructure and defence will have a positive impact

** The broker says it believes the stock can re-rate further as the market gains awareness of the value of the company's components, like its carbon rights and head start on decarbonisation

** It favourably notes Salzgitter's work on cost-cutting and openness to taking over its joint venture HKM from partners Thyssenkrupp and Vallourec

** Shares in Salzgitter seen up 2.2% in early Frankfurt trade
